annotations.label: Banach space

anchor.snippet: complete normed vector spaces over the real or complex numbers

annotations.note: Mathlib encodes Banach spaces as the combination `[NormedSpace 𝕜 E] [CompleteSpace E]` rather than a dedicated class.

annotations.label: Hilbert space

anchor.snippet: An important example is a Hilbert space , where the norm arises from an inner product

annotations.note: Hilbert spaces are encoded as `[InnerProductSpace 𝕜 E] [CompleteSpace E]` with the norm-from-inner-product structure built in.

annotations.label: Classification of Hilbert spaces

anchor.snippet: there is a unique Hilbert space up to isomorphism for every cardinality of the orthonormal basis

annotations.note: `HilbertBasis.repr` provides an isometric isomorphism `E ≃ₗᵢ ℓ²(ι, 𝕜)`, and `exists_hilbertBasis` shows every Hilbert space has one, yielding the classification.

annotations.label: Invariant subspace problem

anchor.snippet: every bounded linear operator on a Hilbert space has a proper invariant subspace

annotations.note: No formalization found — the invariant subspace problem remains an open problem and is not stated in Mathlib.

annotations.label: L^p spaces

anchor.snippet: has as its vectors equivalence classes

annotations.note: `MeasureTheory.Lp E p μ` is defined as equivalence classes of measurable functions with finite Lp norm, with the Banach space structure proved.

annotations.label: Sequence spaces with counting measure

anchor.snippet: is the counting measure , then the integral may be replaced by a sum

annotations.note: `lp E p` is defined directly as a subgroup of dependent sequences with finite p-norm and given its Banach space instance.

annotations.label: Dual space and bidual

anchor.snippet: the space of all continuous linear maps from the space into its underlying field

annotations.note: The dual is `StrongDual 𝕜 E := E →L[𝕜] 𝕜` and the canonical bidual embedding is `NormedSpace.inclusionInDoubleDual`.

annotations.label: Uniform boundedness principle
annotations.note: `banach_steinhaus` proves a pointwise bounded family of continuous linear maps from a complete space is uniformly bounded in operator norm.

annotations.label: Spectral theorem
annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes the finite-dimensional spectral/diagonalization theorem for symmetric operators and a continuous functional calculus for C*-algebras, but no general spectral theorem for bounded self-adjoint operators on Hilbert spaces.

annotations.label: Hahn–Banach theorem
annotations.note: `exists_extension_norm_eq` extends a continuous linear functional from a subspace to the whole normed space preserving norm.

annotations.label: Open mapping theorem
annotations.note: A surjective continuous linear map between Banach spaces is open via `ContinuousLinearMap.isOpenMap`.

annotations.label: Closed graph theorem

anchor.value: Closed graph theorem — If [MATH] is a to

annotations.note: `LinearMap.continuous_of_isClosed_graph` shows a linear map between Banach spaces with closed graph is continuous.
